name: Highlight.io Session Replay & Error Monitoring API
what_it_does: Open-source full-stack observability platform with session replay, error monitoring, logging, and distributed tracing — offering self-hosted deployment or managed cloud with SDKs for frontend session recording and backend error capture.
use_cases: Recording user sessions to debug UI issues alongside frontend errors in unified view
Capturing backend errors and logs correlated with frontend user actions
Self-hosted alternative to LogRocket/Datadog with full data ownership
Querying error and session data programmatically for agent-driven incident response
Distributed tracing across services correlated with user-facing session replay
not_for: Infrastructure-level metrics and alerting (use Prometheus/Grafana for metrics)
Enterprise APM with deep code profiling (use Datadog APM or New Relic)
Teams needing commercial support SLAs without self-hosting ability
best_when: A team needs full-stack observability combining session replay, error tracking, and logging with open-source option and self-hosting capability as privacy-preserving alternative.
avoid_when: You need pure infrastructure monitoring, complex alerting pipelines, or enterprise APM features.

auth.notes: Project API key from Highlight dashboard. Used in SDK initialization and REST API calls. Workspace-scoped access. Self-hosted instances manage their own auth. GraphQL API for data querying.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source — free to self-host. Cloud offers easy setup. Active development with growing feature set. Privacy-friendly — GDPR compliant. Good alternative to closed-source session replay tools.

performance.rate_limits: Based on plan; free tier has session/error quotas

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API for querying sessions and errors programmatically. Multiple language SDKs for integration. Webhook support for error alerts. OpenTelemetry compatible for tracing. Error context includes full stack trace and session replay link.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Error and session reads are idempotent. SDK event ingestion creates new records. API queries always return consistent results for same parameters.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted requires ClickHouse for storage — non-trivial infrastructure setup
Session replay data retention limits apply on free tier
GraphQL API preferred for data queries — REST API more limited
Frontend SDK adds ~50KB to bundle size — verify performance impact for agents

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable. TLS enforced. SOC2 certified on cloud. GDPR compliant. Self-hosted provides complete data control. OpenTelemetry integration follows security standards.
